meta.title: hypo.online — mortgage orientation in the Czech Republic
meta.description: Short check — questionnaire answers are not kept as a profile: they power a one-off AI-style estimate and broker matching (language, case type, complexity). Optional broker contact. Not a bank decision.

nav.guide: Mortgage guide for the Czech Republic

home.badge: Mortgages in the Czech Republic — expat-friendly
home.headline: Two-minute mortgage check: indicative approval odds and a clear next step.
home.sub: This is not a bank decision. An automated pass estimates how lenders might view your file, suggests a practical next step, and can introduce a licensed mortgage broker when that helps.
home.trust1: Questionnaire answers are used only to compute your result, identify the most suitable mortgage broker for your case, and are not stored afterwards.
home.trust2: If you request contact, we keep only name, email and phone for broker handoff.
home.trust3: Broker matching can weigh language, financing goal, and non-standard situations (foreign income, enforcement, non‑EU residency, and similar) — without building a long-term questionnaire dossier.
home.dataCalloutTitle: We do not keep your questionnaire answers
home.dataCalloutBody: hypo.online is built so quick-check data exist only for the live run: the automated model produces an indicative score and, in the same step, supports routing you to the most suitable licensed mortgage broker for your specifics — for example the language you prefer, the product you need, or experience with complex files (enforcement, income from abroad, third-country nationals, and similar). After the session ends, we do not retain the questionnaire payload for profiling.
home.howIntro: hypo.online helps people in Czechia decide whether a mortgage is realistic now — especially foreigners, mixed couples, and self‑employed applicants. The flow is deliberately short: enough signal for brokers, not a 40‑field bank form.
home.how_step1_title: 1) You answer a few focused questions
home.how_step1_body: Typical topics: what you are solving (purchase vs refinance), how your income is structured (employment in CZ, self-employed, foreign employer), and rough timing. Everything is mobile-friendly with one decision per screen.
home.how_step2_title: 2) An AI-style model estimates an approval probability
home.how_step2_body: Our backend runs an automated scoring model (rules today; you can plug in an LLM later). You get an indicative percentage — not a promise — plus the partner broker profile that fits best (language, case shape, complexity). The bank always decides after full documentation.
home.how_step3_title: 3) You see a semaphore: green, yellow, or red
home.how_step3_body: Green suggests a relatively smoother path on average. Yellow and red often mean more documentation, residency, or income-proof complexity — but they are not “stop” signals. Many good files start yellow and finish green after broker structuring.
home.how_step4_title: 4) Yellow and red leads are still processed
home.how_step4_body: We intentionally keep the contact form for every colour. Brokers frequently earn their fee on non-obvious cases. If you want human help, you can always leave your details — the broker team triages urgency and next documents.
home.how_step5_body: If you submit your name, email and phone, we route the lead to partner mortgage brokers in Czechia. They contact you about realistic banks, rates, and paperwork — in line with your language where possible.
home.how_step6_body: We are not a bank and we do not replace regulated advice. Matching can reflect how you prefer to communicate, what you finance, and harder contexts (enforcement, foreign income, non‑EU residency). Treat the output as orientation, then have a professional review your documents.
home.how_lead_note: AI probability + semaphore are marketing-grade summaries. Brokers may decline a lead after KYC — that is normal in lending.
home.heroMicroShort: No obligation. Your answers are used only for this run — expand "How we treat your answers" below for details.
home.dataAccordionSummary: Used only for this check and broker matching — we do not build a long-term profile from your answers.
home.dataAccordionDetails: The model produces an indicative score and suggests the best-matching licensed broker for your specifics (language, product, harder cases such as foreign income or non‑EU residency). After the session ends, we do not retain questionnaire content for remarketing or profiling.
home.valueLead: Quick orientation before banks invest their time — with a broker handoff when you want expertise on tap.
home.valueCoverageBody: We work with mortgage specialists across the CZ/SK market. The right bank always depends on your profile.
home.valueAiBody: One-off scoring from your answers, hints on weak spots, and broker matching by language, goal, and complexity.
home.valueBrokerBody: Optional contact: a broker sanity-checks your documents and maps a realistic plan with a concrete next action.
home.compareR1n: Hard to benchmark banks fairly alone
home.privacyTeaserBody: We do not retain questionnaire content after the session. The full policy covers retention, legal basis, cookies, and your rights.
home.moreToolsFoot: LTV and income topics are also in the guide.
home.schemaFaq1q: Is this a binding bank approval?
home.schemaFaq1a: No. You get an indicative probability and practical next-step guidance, with an optional broker introduction.
home.schemaFaq2a: No. Your answers power only the live calculation and broker-fit suggestion. Afterwards we do not keep them as a stored profile.

quiz.dataPrinciplesIntro: Answers in this check are used only for the live scoring run and to suggest the best-matching licensed broker (language, case type, complexity). We do not keep your questionnaire as a stored profile afterwards.
quiz.intent: What are you mainly looking for?
quiz.timeline: When do you roughly expect to draw the loan?
quiz.aiNotice: This percentage is generated by an automated AI-style scoring model using your answers. It is not a bank decision and can be wrong for your specific documents.
quiz.resultIntro: Our model estimates an indicative approval probability of {percent}%.
quiz.hint_green: On average, similar profiles often proceed with a broker conversation and bank pre-checks without unusual blockers.
quiz.hint_yellow: Similar profiles often need extra documentation or a specialist path — still very workable with a broker.
quiz.hint_red: Similar profiles can be challenging for standard bank scoring — brokers may suggest alternative structures, timing, or lenders. Still worth a call if you are serious.
quiz.allSignalsLead: Yellow and red are not dead ends — submit your contact if you want a human review.
quiz.result_contactTitle: Want a broker to contact you?
quiz.result_contactHint: If you continue, we only persist the contact fields you submit below. Your questionnaire answers were used only for the immediate calculation and broker-fit signal; we do not store them as a long-term profile.
quiz.name: Name
quiz.thankyou: Thank you — a broker will reach out.
quiz.disclaimer: Not a loan offer. Banks decide after full underwriting. The model output is probabilistic and may change as rules change.

quizV2.err_step: Please complete this step before continuing.

A1.title: What kind of mortgage are you planning?
A1.hint: Purchase finances a property buy. Refinance moves an existing housing loan to another bank. American mortgage (CZ) is a non-purpose loan secured on property — banks often cap LTV around 70–80% and scrutinise how you will use the money.

A1b.title: What is the main purpose of the funds?
A1b.hint: Czech banks document the use case for non-purpose mortgages; consolidation and business uses usually need more paperwork.
A1b.debt-consolidation: Pay off / consolidate other loans or cards
A1b.home-renovation: Renovation or construction tied to the pledged property
A1b.investment-liquidity: Liquidity / investment buffer (not tied to the buy)

A2.title: Where is / will the property be located?
A2.hint: Prague centre is typically easiest for bank appetite; other regions may have stricter LTV.

A3.title: What is the estimated property price?
A3.hint: Rough market value in millions of CZK.

A3b.title: Is there another mortgage loan secured on this property?
A3b.hint: An existing senior lien reduces how much a bank will lend on a non-purpose (American) mortgage.
A3b.none: No — no other mortgage on this property
A3b.yes: Yes — another mortgage / bank lien exists
A3b.amount_label: Approximate remaining principal of that loan (CZK)

A3c.title: How much do you plan to draw from the American mortgage?
A3c.hint: Only the disbursement from this American mortgage — other debts are covered in later steps.
A3c.max_note: Rough upper bound used in this step: {max} mil. CZK (from your estimated property value).

A4.title: Roughly what share of the property price will you cover with your own funds?
A4.hint: Approximate share of the purchase price. A larger own-funds share generally improves loan terms; many banks prefer around 20%+.
A4.refi_title: Roughly what equity do you have in the property (value above the mortgage)?
A4.refi_hint: For refinancing, this is the part of the property value that is yours beyond the current loan. More equity usually means better negotiating room.

A5.title: When do you plan to buy / refinance?
A5.hint: Shorter horizons leave less time to prepare documents.

B1.title: What is your main income?
B1.hint: Employment in CZ is usually the simplest path; self-employed and foreign income need more documentation.

B2.employed_title: What is your monthly GROSS salary (CZK)?
B2.employed_hint: Gross = before tax and social/health deductions.
B2.osvcc_title: What is your average monthly income over the last year (CZK)?
B2.osvcc_hint: Banks often average the last two tax returns conservatively.
B2.foreign_title: Monthly income in CZK (after your rough conversion)
B2.foreign_hint: Foreign income usually needs contract and bank statement verification.
B2.mixed_title: Total approximate gross monthly income from all sources (CZK)

B3.employed_title: How long have you worked for your current employer?
B3.osvcc_title: How long have you had your trade licence / self-employment?
B3.foreign_title: How long have you had this foreign income?
B3.hint: Banks often prefer more than 12 months with the same setup.

B4.title: Do you have additional income sources?
B4.hint: Extra documented income can help affordability; passive income is often haircut by banks.

B5.title: How stable do you expect your income to be over the next few years (roughly until your next typical rate-fix period)?
B5.hint: Lenders care whether you can keep up with payments through normal rate changes and life events—not a multi-decade career forecast.

C1.title: Do you have instalment loans (car, personal, student)?
C1.hint: Higher existing payments reduce room for a mortgage (DTI).

C2.title: Credit cards — what is your situation?
C2.hint: High utilisation hurts scoring; paying in full each month is better.
C2.undebt: I have cards and pay them off monthly

C3.title: Other monthly financial obligations?
C3.hint: Alimony counts fully in affordability; enforcement is a serious issue.

C4.title: Any past payment issues?
C4.hint: Czech banks check credit bureaus; very recent problems weigh heavily.

C5.title: What is your status in Czechia?
C5.hint: Permanent residence is usually simplest; thin residency adds documentation and risk.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.p1: We process personal data transparently and only to the extent necessary for the hypo.online service. Questionnaire answers for the quick check are used solely for the on-the-spot automated evaluation and broker matching; we do not retain them as a lasting profile after that run. Optional lead handoff uses only the contact details you choose to send. We also comply with legal obligations.
privacy.p2: This page summarises key GDPR-related information. It does not replace individual legal advice. For questions about your data, write to hello@hypo.online.
privacy.controller_body:
The controller of personal data processed in connection with the hypo.online website and related services is mareke solutions s.r.o., Company ID (IČO): 29415675, with its registered office at Korunní 2569/108B, Vinohrady, 101 00 Prague, Czech Republic.

For exercising your rights or privacy questions, use the email address hello@hypo.online.
privacy.purposes_body:
• Operating the website and the indicative mortgage check (questionnaire answers used only for the immediate AI-style evaluation and to identify the most suitable licensed mortgage broker for your case — e.g. language, financing goal, or non-standard situations; not stored as a long-term profile).
• Optional processing of contact details you voluntarily submit so a partner mortgage broker can contact you.
• Compliance with legal obligations applicable to the controller (accounting, tax, responses to regulators or courts where relevant).
privacy.legal_body: Depending on the situation, we rely on: performance of a contract or pre-contractual steps at your request (Article 6(1)(b) GDPR); compliance with a legal obligation (Article 6(1)(c)); legitimate interests such as fraud prevention, IT security, or improving the service, where not overridden by your interests (Article 6(1)(f)); and consent where required, e.g. for non-essential cookies or certain marketing analytics (Article 6(1)(a)).
privacy.retention_body: Data are kept only as long as necessary for the given purpose. Indicative questionnaire data used solely for the live calculation and broker matching are not kept as a permanent customer dossier after the session ends. Contact details provided for broker handoff are kept for the period necessary to respond and to document the relationship, in line with internal policies and law. Technical logs may be kept for a limited security window.
privacy.recipients_body: Personal data may be disclosed to: partner mortgage brokers where you request contact; IT and hosting providers that process data on documented instructions (processors); and authorities where the law requires it. We do not sell your personal data.
privacy.transfers_body: If tools with a non-EEA component are used (e.g. certain cloud or analytics providers), transfers take place on the basis of appropriate safeguards under GDPR (such as Standard Contractual Clauses) and provider documentation. You may request more detail via hello@hypo.online.
privacy.rights_body:
Under the GDPR and, where applicable, Czech Act No. 110/2019 Coll. (personal data processing), you may request: access to your data; rectification of inaccurate data; erasure (“right to be forgotten”) where conditions are met; restriction of processing; data portability where processing is based on consent or contract and automated; objection to processing based on legitimate interests; and withdrawal of consent where processing is consent-based (without affecting prior lawful processing).

Where the service offers a self-service deletion action after you submit a lead, you can use that flow in addition to emailing hello@hypo.online.
privacy.cookies_body: Essential cookies may be used to run the site. Optional analytics or marketing cookies are used only with your consent via the cookie banner or settings where available. You can change browser settings to block cookies, which may limit some features.
privacy.complaint_body: If you believe processing infringes the law, you may lodge a complaint with the Czech Office for Personal Data Protection (Úřad pro ochranu osobních údajů), Pplk. Sochora 27, 170 00 Prague 7, www.uoou.cz.
privacy.dataflow_b1: Questionnaire data for the quick check: used only for the immediate evaluation and to identify the most suitable licensed mortgage broker for your specifics (e.g. language, financing goal, enforcement, foreign income, non‑EU residency, and similar); we do not store them after this calculation.
privacy.dataflow_b2: Contact data (name, e-mail, phone if provided): used to pass your request to a partner mortgage broker and for related follow-up.
privacy.dataflow_b3: Analytics: the questionnaire flow does not ask for or record analytics consent.
privacy.dataflow_b4: Deletion: where the product allows, you may request deletion from the result view after submitting contact details; you can always contact hello@hypo.online.
